reason: Both answers correctly diagnose the Goodhart's-law dynamic and converge on the same core fix (incident frequency/total impact-minutes, SLO/error-budget burn, p90/p95, recurrence rate, pager-load proxies). Answer A adds one genuinely new, substantive point: MTTR is statistically noisy over small quarterly samples (citing the VOID report), which changes how the asker should interpret the 40% figure itself, plus minor extras (DORA change-failure-rate, qualitative survey). Answer B is cleaner in tone but adds nothing beyond A. A's extra content is real signal, not padding, giving it a slight edge.
